violations.description: Assertion for thread ID stability is guarded by a conditional branch, so the key invariant can be skipped.
violations.suggestion: Assert non-null ID first, then assert exact stability without conditional branching.

violations.description: Mock response path uses in-test branching by dispatch attempt count.
violations.suggestion: Prefer explicit dedicated tests or deterministic route fixtures per scenario.

violations.description: Correlation IDs are randomized, which can reduce replayability during flaky triage.
violations.suggestion: Allow deterministic correlation ID override from testInfo-based helper.

violations.description: CSRF token defaults are randomized in factory output.
violations.suggestion: Use deterministic token helper for repeatable debug reproduction when needed.

recommendations: Remove conditional assertion gating for thread-id continuity checks
Use deterministic fixture IDs/tokens in debug or CI replay mode
Keep envelope-mock branches isolated by test scenario for clearer failure diagnosis
summary: Determinism is strong overall; minor branching/randomization patterns can be tightened for easier replay debugging.
